Home › Guides

Money guides & explainers

35 plain-English guides to the money decisions that matter — no jargon, no sales pitch, just the reasoning and the numbers.

No guides match — try another word.

"What Is a 401(k) Match, and Why It's the Best Deal in Finance" How employer 401(k) matching works, why skipping it means leaving free money on the table, and how vesting and contribution limits affect you. "The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. "APR vs APY: The Difference That Costs (or Earns) You Money" APR and APY look alike but mean different things. Understand compounding, why lenders quote APR and banks quote APY, and how to compare fairly. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "What Actually Affects Your Credit Score" The five factors behind your credit score, which ones matter most, and the practical habits that raise it — without the myths. "Do You Need Life Insurance? Term vs Whole Life, Explained" Who actually needs life insurance, how much, and why term insurance beats whole life for most people. A clear guide to a confusing, heavily-sold product. "Dollar-Cost Averaging: Why Boring, Automatic Investing Wins" How dollar-cost averaging works, what the research actually says versus lump-sum investing, and why the biggest benefit is behavioral, not mathematical. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "The Financial Order of Operations: What to Do With Your Money First" A clear, prioritized sequence for every spare dollar — emergency fund, employer match, high-interest debt, investing — so you never wonder what comes next. "The FIRE Movement Explained: Financial Independence by the Numbers" What FIRE really means, the 25x rule and 4% withdrawal, the variants (Lean, Fat, Coast, Barista FIRE), and the one number that decides your timeline. "Good Debt vs Bad Debt: A Simple Framework for Borrowing Decisions" Not all debt is equal. A practical framework for telling wealth-building debt from wealth-destroying debt, using interest rate, asset value and cash flow. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "How Much Should You Save for Retirement?" Rules of thumb, the real math behind retirement targets, and how to turn a scary abstract goal into a monthly number you can actually act on. "How Much Should You Spend on a Car? Rules of Thumb That Actually Work" Practical guidelines for a sensible car budget — the 20/4/10 rule, why total cost dwarfs the sticker price, and how a car quietly drains wealth. "How to Build Credit From Scratch (and Fix a Low Score)" A practical, step-by-step guide to building a credit history from zero and raising a low score — what actually moves the needle and what to ignore. "How to Handle a Market Crash Without Wrecking Your Finances" What to do — and what never to do — when markets fall. Why crashes are normal, why selling locks in losses, and how to turn a downturn to your advantage. "How to Make a Budget That Actually Sticks" A practical guide to budgeting that survives real life — the main methods compared, how to set one up in an afternoon, and why most budgets fail. "How to Negotiate a Raise (Without the Awkwardness)" A practical, evidence-based guide to asking for a raise — how to build your case, what number to name, the words to use, and why it compounds over a career. "Index Funds Explained: Why the Boring Choice Beats the Pros" What an index fund is, why it quietly outperforms most active managers after fees, and how to use one as the core of a simple, low-cost portfolio. "Lifestyle Creep: Why Earning More Doesn't Make You Richer" How lifestyle inflation quietly eats every raise, why high earners can still live paycheck to paycheck, and a simple rule to keep more of what you earn. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "Why Net Worth Is the Only Number That Really Matters" Income tells you how fast you're rowing; net worth tells you where the boat actually is. How to calculate it, track it, and use it to build wealth. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament. "How to Price Freelance Work Without Underselling Yourself" Why new freelancers charge too little, how to calculate a rate that actually sustains you, and when to move from hourly to value-based pricing. "Renting vs Buying a Home: The Honest Math" Beyond 'rent is throwing money away' — the real costs on both sides, the break-even horizon, and how to decide based on your actual situation. "Roth vs Traditional: Which Retirement Account Wins?" A clear framework for choosing between Roth and Traditional retirement accounts — the tax logic, the break-even rule, and why many people use both. "How to Save for a House Down Payment (Without It Taking Forever)" How much to save for a down payment, where to keep it, and practical tactics to get there faster — plus why 20% isn't always the right target. "Sequence of Returns Risk: Why When You Retire Matters as Much as How Much" The retirement danger hiding inside average returns — why a crash early in retirement is far worse than the same crash later, and how to defend against it. "Sinking Funds: The Simple Trick for Big Expenses" How sinking funds turn irregular, budget-wrecking expenses into small predictable monthly savings — and why they beat relying on your emergency fund. "How to Start Investing With Little Money: A Beginner's Roadmap" You don't need thousands to start investing. A practical, jargon-free roadmap for beginners — what to do first, what to buy, and the mistakes to skip. "Stocks vs Bonds: How to Think About Asset Allocation" What stocks and bonds actually are, how they behave differently, and how to choose a stock/bond mix that matches your timeline and stomach for risk. "The Time Value of Money: Why a Dollar Today Beats a Dollar Tomorrow" The single most important idea in finance, explained plainly — present value, future value, discounting, and why it underpins every investment and loan decision. "The True Cost of Owning a Car (It's Not the Payment)" Depreciation, insurance, fuel, maintenance and financing — the full cost of car ownership, and how to spend far less on getting around. "Understanding Inflation: How It Quietly Shrinks Your Money" What inflation is, why central banks target it, how it erodes cash and helps borrowers, and how to protect your money from losing value over time. "When Does Refinancing Your Mortgage Make Sense?" How to tell whether refinancing will save you money — the break-even rule, the costs people forget, and the traps that make a lower rate a worse deal.
FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
